reasoning: The stock recently released disappointing Q4 earnings on October 28 with a revenue miss and cautious fiscal 2026 guidance, driving shares down sharply. Although the longer-term base rate is positive, the stock's short-term momentum has been decidedly downward, drifting lower in the days leading to the November 9 cutoff. Additionally, the company announced a $0.45 dividend for shareholders of record on November 13. The ex-dividend date will fall on November 12, meaning the stock will face an automatic drop of roughly $0.45 before the first resolution date (November 16). This ex-dividend action, combined with the negative post-earnings sentiment, pushes the very short-term probability below 0.5. Following the structural guideline that uncertainty increases closer to 0.5 over longer horizons, the forecasts progressively step up to an uninformative 0.5 over the distant dates.
